Just now

--

Why the Next Competitive Advantage May Not Be Better AI, But Better Organisations
For the past few years, the conversation around artificial intelligence has been remarkably consistent.
How do we use AI more effectively?
Learn prompting.
Adopt new models.
Automate repetitive work.
Increase productivity.
Those capabilities matter.
They are changing the way individuals work and creating enormous value.
But they were never the final destination.
As AI models become faster, cheaper and more coherent, the bottleneck begins to move.
Not to the model.
To the organisation.
A New Property
I increasingly believe every organisation possesses a property that will become more important in the age of AI.
I call it Organisational Coherence.
Organisational Coherence is the degree to which an organisation’s people, product, processes and digital footprint tell AI systems one consistent story.
At first glance this sounds abstract.
In practice, it is surprisingly tangible.
Do leaders communicate the same priorities?
Do teams make decisions using the same principles?
Do products reflect the company’s stated purpose?
Do internal processes reinforce rather than contradict one another?
Does the organisation’s digital footprint accurately represent how it actually operates?
Every organisation answers these questions, whether intentionally or not.
The difference is that coherent organisations answer them consistently.
The Organisation Becomes the System
An AI model doesn’t simply answer questions.
Increasingly, it attempts to model how organisations work.
Who makes decisions.
Which information can be trusted.
How different parts of the organisation relate to one another.
Whether previous solutions can be reused.
Every interaction contributes to an internal representation.
Like people, AI performs better when that representation is clear.
The easier an organisation is to understand, the less uncertainty the system faces.
Confidence emerges when uncertainty falls.
And lower uncertainty almost always leads to better outcomes.
Coherence Creates Legibility
Every organisation emits signals.
Leadership decisions.
Processes.
Documentation.
Products.
Customer interactions.
Content.
Culture.
Traditionally these have been treated as separate functions.
Marketing owns one.
Operations another.
Leadership another.
Technology another.
AI doesn’t experience them separately.
It experiences the organisation as a whole.
If those signals reinforce one another, the organisation becomes easier to interpret.
Not only for people.
Increasingly, for AI systems as well.
Organisational coherence creates organisational legibility.
The Economics Are Changing
One of the most important trends in AI is that intelligence is becoming cheaper.
Inference costs continue to fall.
Models continue to improve.
Capabilities continue to expand.
As that happens, intelligence itself becomes increasingly abundant.
The scarce resource shifts.
Not intelligence.
The ability to organise intelligence.
The organisations that reduce the effort required to understand them gain an advantage.
Less ambiguity.
Less duplication.
More reusable decisions.
Greater consistency.
Higher confidence.
AI systems spend less effort reconstructing context and more effort creating value.
Beyond AI Adoption
The first phase of AI transformation focused on helping people use AI.
The next phase may focus on helping organisations become understandable to AI.
That is a fundamentally different challenge.
It isn’t solved by choosing another model.
Or writing better prompts.
It requires aligning leadership, processes, products and digital signals into one coherent system.
The technology continues to improve.
The organisation must evolve alongside it.
A New Competitive Advantage
For decades we optimised organisations for human collaboration.
Clear communication.
Shared purpose.
Aligned teams.
Effective processes.
Those qualities remain valuable.
But they now create a second advantage.
They make organisations easier for AI systems to model.
Easier to trust.
Easier to recommend.
Ultimately, easier to work with.
The companies that embrace this won’t simply use AI more effectively.
They’ll become organisations that intelligence itself can work with more efficiently.
Looking Ahead
Every major technological shift changes where value is created.
The internet rewarded discoverability.
Search rewarded relevance.
Social media rewarded attention.
AI may increasingly reward coherence.
Not because coherence is fashionable.
Because coherent organisations are easier to understand.
And understanding reduces uncertainty.
As intelligence becomes abundant, reducing uncertainty may become one of the most valuable capabilities an organisation can develop.
That is why I believe organisational coherence is becoming a strategic advantage.
Not just for people.
But for intelligence itself.
⸻
Closing Thought
The first generation of AI transformed how people work.
The next generation may transform how organisations think.
The winners won’t necessarily have the most powerful models.
They’ll build the most coherent organisations.
Because in the age of AI, intelligence increasingly flows towards coherence.
